Extract field names from a form schema with type safety.
Useful for creating type-safe field name constants.

content.text:
```tsx
type FormValues = { email: string; password: string; nested: { field: string } }
type FieldName = FieldName<FormValues> // 'email' | 'password' | 'nested' | 'nested.field'
```

summary.text:
Flex/Grid alignment options
Used by Popover, charts, and layout components
Form wrapper that provides react-hook-form context to child components.

content.text:
```tsx
const form = useForm({ resolver: zodResolver(schema) })

<Form form={form} onSubmit={form.handleSubmit(onSubmit)}>
  <FormField control={form.control} name="email" render={({ field }) => (
    <FormItem>
      <FormLabel>Email</FormLabel>
      <FormControl><Input {...field} /></FormControl>
      <FormMessage />
    </FormItem>
  )} />
</Form>
```

Creates a Chart.js external tooltip handler matching the MDK design.

The tooltip follows the real mouse cursor (not data points) via a mousemove
listener on the chart container. Content is updated by Chart.js's external
tooltip callback; positioning is driven entirely by the mouse.

Calculate curtailment in MWh and curtailment rate for sites

Curtailment represents the difference between nominal available power and actual used energy.

Formulas:
-
`powerConsumptionInMWh = powerConsumptionMW * hoursInPeriod`
`curtailmentMWh = nominalAvailablePowerMWh - usedEnergyInMWh`
`curtailmentRate = curtailmentMWh / powerConsumptionInMWh`
If curtailment rate is negative (used energy exceeds nominal available), it is clamped to 0.
The actual used energy in Wh
The nominal available power in MWh for the period
The power consumption in MW
The number of hours in the period

Generator that infinitely cycles through array elements

content.text:
```ts
const colors = circularArrayAccess(['red', 'blue', 'green'])
colors.next().value  // 'red'
colors.next().value  // 'blue'
colors.next().value  // 'green'
colors.next().value  // 'red' (wraps around)
```

Create conditional validation where one field is required if another field has a specific value.

content.text:
```tsx
const formSchema = z.object({
  type: z.enum(['email', 'phone']),
  email: z.string().optional(),
  phone: z.string().optional(),
})
  .refine(...createConditionalRequired('type', 'email', 'email', 'Email is required'))
  .refine(...createConditionalRequired('type', 'phone', 'phone', 'Phone is required'))
```

```tsx
  control={form.control}
  name="priority"

  options={[
    { value: 'low', label: 'Low' },
    { value: 'medium', label: 'Medium' },
    { value: 'high', label: 'High' },
  ]}
  orientation="horizontal"
/>
```

```tsx
<FormSelect

    { value: 'user', label: 'User' },

  description="Your access level"
/>
```

LazyTabWrapper - Wrapper for lazy-loaded tab components with Suspense

Handles lazy loading with a fallback spinner while the component loads.
Useful for code-splitting large tab components.

Mosaic - Grid layout component with named areas

Features:
- Named grid areas for layout
- Responsive: stacks on mobile, grid on desktop
- Auto-generates column tracks
- Validates column count vs template

content.text:
```tsx
<Mosaic
  template={[
    ['header', 'header'],
    ['sidebar', 'content'],
    ['footer', 'footer']
  ]}
  gap="16px"
  rowHeight="auto"
>
  <Mosaic.Item area="header">Header</Mosaic.Item>
  <Mosaic.Item area="sidebar">Sidebar</Mosaic.Item>
  <Mosaic.Item area="content">Content</Mosaic.Item>
  <Mosaic.Item area="footer">Footer</Mosaic.Item>
</Mosaic>
```

```tsx
<Mosaic
  template={[
    'header header header',
    'nav    main   aside',
    'footer footer footer'
  ]}
>
  <Mosaic.Item area="header">Header</Mosaic.Item>
  <Mosaic.Item area="nav">Nav</Mosaic.Item>
  <Mosaic.Item area="main">Main</Mosaic.Item>
  <Mosaic.Item area="aside">Aside</Mosaic.Item>
  <Mosaic.Item area="footer">Footer</Mosaic.Item>
</Mosaic>
```
